changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Letter From Madoka"

TextENGNew: ...Greetings.
How are you doing?
As usual, we're doing fine in this lovely weather.
The people in the village were a little aloof when we first moved here, but we've become good friends now.
A lot of people came to celebrate Kaoru-san's 60th birthday last month.
...Oh, that's right!
Kaoru-san was really embarrassed about it, but we dressed him in a red chanchanko jacket, like you do at a 60th birthday!
I half-jokingly told him, ``It'll last a lifetime, right?`` when he put it on, but it actually looked pretty good on him.
We had a good laugh about it.
I'll try to send a picture next time, ok?
I also packaged some fresh summer oranges along with this letter. We harvested them this year, so please enjoy them together.
I've finally become older than Sis was.
...It's a shame that my children never had an opportunity to call someone ``Aunt``.
But surprisingly, turning 30 wasn't as bad I expected.
...Does it upset you to hear that, Akasaka-san?
...These days, I've finally been able to stand over Sis's grave without crying.
I originally wished that her grave was closer to Mom and Dad's, but now I'm starting to think bringing her grave over here was the right decision.
Sis was always working her hardest, going forward at full power without taking a moment to breathe.
But now that she's out here in the middle of this lovely nature, she finally has no choice but to rest. That's what I believe.
...Oh, but if there are police officers in heaven, maybe she's running around up there now?
Also, Akasaka-san.
Going forward, please don't blame yourself for what happened to Sis.
I told Kura-chan the same thing, too.
It's not anybody's fault.
Sis was just carrying out her duties, so it's wrong to blame anyone other than the culprit. It was the nature of her position. I want the two of you to understand that.
Instead, if you could redirect those feelings toward solving other cases and keeping the perpetrators off the streets... that would make me happy.
Sometimes, Kaoru-san claps his hands together and apologizes at her grave, saying that his inability to catch the culprit during his tenure at the station is his biggest regret.
(...And each time, I'd smack him as hard as I could!)
However, thanks to the hard work of Fujita-san and the rest of the team, they were finally able to catch the suspect last year.
So, I think Sis would be grateful to know that everyone did their best and refused to give up.
So from now on, please do your best at work.

My family and I pray that you become Superintendent General of the Metropolitan Police... well, perhaps that's too much of a stretch, but we hope you reach a position close to it and continue working for world peace.
So, please take care of yourself.
Talk to you later.
Madoka Yamaoki.
